chunks.text: Robert G. Meyer is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. His contact information includes office at 505 Cory Hall, phone 510-642-8026, and email rmeyer@eecs.berkeley.edu. Office hours are by appointment only at 505 Cory Hall.

Professor Meyer received his Bachelor of Electrical Engineering (First Class Honors) in 1963 from University of Melbourne, Master of Engineering Science (Honors) in 1965 for thesis on transistor noise parameters, and Ph.D. in 1968 for thesis on transistor mixer noise. He received the J.J Thomson Premium in 1968 for mixer noise research.

From January to September 1968, Meyer was Assistant Lecturer at University of Melbourne. Since September 1968, he has been at UC Berkeley's EECS department, now as National Semiconductor Distinguished Professor Emeritus. His research focuses on integrated-circuit design, device fabrication, nonlinear phenomena, and noise performance.

Meyer co-authored 'Analysis and Design of Analog Integrated Circuits' (Wiley editions 1977-2001), edited 'Integrated Circuit Operational Amplifiers' (IEEE Press 1978), and co-edited 'Integrated Circuits for Wireless Communications' (IEEE Press 1999). He has consulted for numerous electronics companies and was past President of IEEE Solid-State Circuits Council.

Meyer was Guest Editor of IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits in 1973, 1976, 1987, and Associate Editor from 1976-1982. He was Associate Editor of IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems. He was Visiting Professor at Catholic University of Leuven (1975) and Columbia University (1996, 2003).

Meyer received IEEE Leon K. Kirchmayer Graduate Teaching Award in 2003 and IEEE Donald O. Pederson Award in 2014 for analog/RF circuit contributions. He holds eight U.S. patents and is IEEE Fellow. His research area is Integrated Circuits (INC).
